title: Christmas Date Formatter
description: Import the datetime module. Create a date object for **Christmas 2024** (December 25, 2024). Then print that date in the format **12/25/2024** using strftime.
starter_code:
# Import the datetime module
# Create a date for December 25, 2024
# Print it formatted as 12/25/2024

code_requirements.fail_message: Import the datetime module with 'import datetime' or 'from datetime import ...'.

code_requirements.description: Uses the strftime method to format the date
code_requirements.fail_message: Use the strftime method to format the date instead of printing a hardcoded string.

hints: Use datetime.date(2024, 12, 25) to create the date object
Call strftime on the date object with the format string '%m/%d/%Y'
Pass the result directly to print(): print(christmas.strftime('%m/%d/%Y'))
